message:
feat: add 5 orbit maintenance mission templates (#1980)

Add recurring orbit mission templates for the mission browser:
- orbit-health-check: Weekly pod/service health verification
- orbit-cert-rotation: Monthly TLS certificate expiry check
- orbit-version-drift: Weekly Helm/image version drift detection
- orbit-resource-quota: Daily namespace quota monitoring
- orbit-backup-verification: Weekly backup job verification

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "orbit-backup-verification",
+  "missionClass": "orbit",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Weekly Backup Job Verification",
+    "description": "Recurring orbit mission that verifies backup CronJobs are running successfully. Checks Velero backups, CronJob completion status, and PersistentVolume snapshot health to ensure disaster recovery readiness.",
+    "type": "maintain",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 10,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check CronJob health and recent completions",
+        "description": "List all CronJobs and verify they have completed successfully in their expected schedule window.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get cronjobs -A -o custom-columns='NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,SCHEDULE:.spec.schedule,SUSPENDED:.spec.suspend,ACTIVE:.status.active,LAST_SCHEDULE:.status.lastScheduleTime,LAST_SUCCESS:.status.lastSuccessfulTime'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get cronjobs -A -o json | jq '[.items[] | select(.spec.suspend != true) | select(.status.lastSuccessfulTime == null)] | length'",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Detect failed or stuck Jobs from CronJobs",
+        "description": "Find Jobs created by CronJobs that have failed or are running beyond their expected duration.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get jobs -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.failed > 0) | \"FAILED: \\(.metadata.namespace)/\\(.metadata.name) failures=\\(.status.failed)\"'",
+          "kubectl get jobs -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.active > 0) | select(.status.startTime) | \"RUNNING: \\(.metadata.namespace)/\\(.metadata.name) since=\\(.status.startTime)\"'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify Velero backup status",
+        "description": "Check Velero backup schedules and recent backup completion status. Skip if Velero is not installed.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get backupschedules.velero.io -A 2>/dev/null || echo 'Velero not installed — skipping'",
+          "kubectl get backups.velero.io -A --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp 2>/dev/null | tail -10 || echo 'No Velero backups found'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get backups.velero.io -A -o json 2>/dev/null | jq '[.items[-5:] | .[] | select(.status.phase != \"Completed\")] | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check VolumeSnapshot health",
+        "description": "Verify PersistentVolume snapshots are in a ready state and not accumulating errors.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get volumesnapshots -A -o custom-columns='NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyToUse,SIZE:.status.restoreSize,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp' 2>/dev/null || echo 'VolumeSnapshot CRDs not installed'",
+          "kubectl get volumesnapshots -A -o json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.readyToUse != true) | \"NOT READY: \\(.metadata.namespace)/\\(.metadata.name)\"' 2>/dev/null"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review backup-related events and errors",
+        "description": "Check for recent events related to backup failures, volume snapshot errors, or CronJob problems.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=BackoffLimitExceeded --sort-by=.lastTimestamp 2>/dev/null | tail -10",
+          "kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=FailedCreate --sort-by=.lastTimestamp 2>/dev/null | tail -10"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Backup verification complete. All CronJobs, Velero backups, and VolumeSnapshots have been checked for health and successful completion.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get cronjobs -A",
+        "kubectl get backups.velero.io -A"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": ["orbit", "maintenance", "recurring", "backup", "disaster-recovery", "velero", "cronjobs"],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "operations",
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": ["maintenance"]
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": ["kubectl", "jq"],
+    "description": "Requires kubectl with cluster access and jq for JSON parsing. Velero and VolumeSnapshot CRDs are optional but checked if present."
+  },
+  "orbitConfig": {
+    "cadence": "weekly",
+    "orbitType": "backup-verification"
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,85 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "orbit-cert-rotation",
+  "missionClass": "orbit",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Monthly TLS Certificate Expiry Check",
+    "description": "Recurring orbit mission that scans for TLS certificates approaching expiration. Checks cert-manager Certificate resources, Kubernetes TLS secrets, and ingress certificates to prevent unexpected outages from expired certs.",
+    "type": "maintain",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 15,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check cert-manager Certificate resources",
+        "description": "List all cert-manager Certificate resources and their renewal status. Certificates with Ready=False or approaching notAfter dates need attention.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get certificates -A -o wide 2>/dev/null || echo 'cert-manager CRDs not installed'",
+          "kubectl get certificates -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name} notAfter={.status.notAfter} ready={range .status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Ready\")]}{.status}{end}{\"\\n\"}{end}' 2>/dev/null"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get certificates -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Ready\")]}{.status}{\"\\n\"}{end}{end}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c False || echo 0",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Scan TLS secrets for expiring certificates",
+        "description": "Extract and check expiration dates from all TLS-type secrets in the cluster. Flag any expiring within 30 days.",
+        "commands": [
+          "for secret in $(kubectl get secrets -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@.type==\"kubernetes.io/tls\")]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name}{\"\\n\"}{end}'); do ns=$(echo $secret | cut -d/ -f1); name=$(echo $secret | cut -d/ -f2); expiry=$(kubectl get secret $name -n $ns -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -enddate 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); echo \"$secret expires: $expiry\"; done"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check ingress TLS configuration",
+        "description": "Verify that all ingresses with TLS blocks reference valid, non-expired secrets.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get ingress -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name}: {range .spec.tls[*]}secret={.secretName} hosts={.hosts[*]} {end}{\"\\n\"}{end}'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify cert-manager issuer health",
+        "description": "Check that ClusterIssuers and Issuers are in a Ready state so new certificates can be issued when renewals are needed.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get clusterissuers -o wide 2>/dev/null || echo 'No ClusterIssuers found'",
+          "kubectl get issuers -A -o wide 2>/dev/null || echo 'No Issuers found'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get clusterissuers -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Ready\")]}{.status}{\"\\n\"}{end}{end}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c False || echo 0",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review certificate events for renewal failures",
+        "description": "Check for recent cert-manager events that indicate failed renewals or issuance problems.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=RenewalFailed --sort-by=.lastTimestamp 2>/dev/null | tail -10",
+          "kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=IssuerNotReady --sort-by=.lastTimestamp 2>/dev/null | tail -10"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "TLS certificate expiry check complete. All certificates, secrets, and issuers have been reviewed for upcoming expirations and health status.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get certificates -A -o wide",
+        "kubectl get clusterissuers -o wide"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": ["orbit", "maintenance", "recurring", "certificates", "tls", "cert-manager", "security"],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "operations",
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": ["maintenance"]
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": ["kubectl", "openssl"],
+    "description": "Requires kubectl with cluster access. openssl is needed to decode certificate expiry dates. cert-manager CRDs are optional but recommended."
+  },
+  "orbitConfig": {
+    "cadence": "monthly",
+    "orbitType": "cert-rotation"
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "orbit-health-check",
+  "missionClass": "orbit",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Weekly Pod & Service Health Check",
+    "description": "Recurring orbit mission that verifies pod and service health across all namespaces. Detects crash-looping containers, pending pods, and services with no healthy endpoints. Run weekly to catch issues before they affect users.",
+    "type": "maintain",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 10,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check for non-running pods",
+        "description": "List all pods that are not in a Running or Succeeded state. These may be crash-looping, pending, or stuck in an error state.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -A --field-selector status.phase!=Running,status.phase!=Succeeded --no-headers"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get pods -A --field-selector status.phase!=Running,status.phase!=Succeeded --no-headers | wc -l",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Detect containers with high restart counts",
+        "description": "Identify pods with containers that have restarted more than 5 times, indicating potential crash-loop or OOM issues.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name} restarts={range .status.containerStatuses[*]}{.restartCount} {end}{\"\\n\"}{end}' | awk '{for(i=2;i<=NF;i++) if($i+0 > 5) print $0}'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get pods -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .status.containerStatuses[*]}{.restartCount}{\"\\n\"}{end}{end}' | awk '$1 > 5' | wc -l",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check for services with no endpoints",
+        "description": "Find services that have no backing endpoints, which means traffic sent to these services will fail.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get endpoints -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select((.subsets // []) | length == 0) | \"\\(.metadata.namespace)/\\(.metadata.name)\"'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get endpoints -A -o json | jq '[.items[] | select((.subsets // []) | length == 0)] | length'",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify node readiness",
+        "description": "Ensure all nodes in the cluster are in Ready condition and not under resource pressure.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get nodes -o wide",
+          "kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {range .status.conditions[*]}{.type}={.status} {end}{\"\\n\"}{end}'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get nodes --no-headers | grep -v ' Ready' | wc -l",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check recent warning events",
+        "description": "Review warning-level events from the last hour that may indicate emerging problems.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get events -A --field-selector type=Warning --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Pod and service health check complete. All non-running pods, high-restart containers, and endpoint-less services have been identified.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get pods -A --field-selector status.phase!=Running",
+        "kubectl get events -A --field-selector type=Warning"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": ["orbit", "maintenance", "recurring", "health-check", "pods", "services"],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "operations",
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": ["maintenance"]
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": ["kubectl", "jq"],
+    "description": "Requires kubectl with cluster access and jq for JSON parsing."
+  },
+  "orbitConfig": {
+    "cadence": "weekly",
+    "orbitType": "health-check"
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "orbit-resource-quota",
+  "missionClass": "orbit",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Daily Namespace Resource Quota Monitoring",
+    "description": "Recurring orbit mission that monitors namespace resource quotas and limit ranges. Detects namespaces approaching their CPU, memory, or pod limits to prevent scheduling failures and resource exhaustion.",
+    "type": "maintain",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 5,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "List all resource quotas and utilization",
+        "description": "Show all ResourceQuota objects with their current usage vs. hard limits across all namespaces.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get resourcequotas -A -o custom-columns='NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,CPU_USED:.status.used.requests\\.cpu,CPU_HARD:.status.hard.requests\\.cpu,MEM_USED:.status.used.requests\\.memory,MEM_HARD:.status.hard.requests\\.memory,PODS_USED:.status.used.pods,PODS_HARD:.status.hard.pods'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Detect quotas above 80% utilization",
+        "description": "Identify resource quotas where usage exceeds 80% of the hard limit, indicating namespaces at risk of hitting their ceiling.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get resourcequotas -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | .metadata.namespace as $ns | .metadata.name as $name | .status | to_entries[] | select(.key | startswith(\"used.\")) | {ns: $ns, name: $name, resource: (.key | ltrimstr(\"used.\")), used: .value} + ({hard: (.key | ltrimstr(\"used.\") | . as $r | input_line_number)} | {}) | \"\\($ns)/\\($name) \\(.resource): used=\\(.used)\"'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check for namespaces without quotas",
+        "description": "List namespaces that have no ResourceQuota configured, which could allow unbounded resource consumption.",
+        "commands": [
+          "comm -23 <(kubectl get namespaces -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{\"\\n\"}{end}' | sort) <(kubectl get resourcequotas -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}{\"\\n\"}{end}' | sort -u)"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "comm -23 <(kubectl get namespaces -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{\"\\n\"}{end}' | grep -v '^kube-' | sort) <(kubectl get resourcequotas -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}{\"\\n\"}{end}' | sort -u) | wc -l",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review LimitRange defaults",
+        "description": "Show all LimitRange objects to verify default CPU and memory limits are set for containers that do not specify their own.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get limitranges -A -o custom-columns='NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.spec.limits[*].type,DEFAULT_CPU:.spec.limits[*].default.cpu,DEFAULT_MEM:.spec.limits[*].default.memory,MAX_CPU:.spec.limits[*].max.cpu,MAX_MEM:.spec.limits[*].max.memory'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check for pods with no resource requests",
+        "description": "Identify pods that do not set CPU or memory requests, which makes quota enforcement and scheduling unpredictable.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.containers[] | (.resources.requests.cpu == null) or (.resources.requests.memory == null)) | \"\\(.metadata.namespace)/\\(.metadata.name)\"' | head -20"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Resource quota monitoring complete. All namespace quotas, limit ranges, and pod resource requests have been reviewed. Namespaces approaching their limits have been identified.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get resourcequotas -A",
+        "kubectl get limitranges -A"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": ["orbit", "maintenance", "recurring", "resource-quota", "capacity", "namespaces", "limits"],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "operations",
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": ["maintenance"]
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": ["kubectl", "jq"],
+    "description": "Requires kubectl with cluster access and jq for JSON parsing."
+  },
+  "orbitConfig": {
+    "cadence": "daily",
+    "orbitType": "resource-quota"
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,83 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "orbit-version-drift",
+  "missionClass": "orbit",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Weekly Helm & Image Version Drift Detection",
+    "description": "Recurring orbit mission that detects version drift across Helm releases and container images. Identifies outdated Helm charts, pinned-but-stale image tags, and inconsistent versions across namespaces to keep your cluster current.",
+    "type": "maintain",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 10,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "List all Helm releases and their chart versions",
+        "description": "Enumerate every Helm release in the cluster along with its chart version, app version, and status to establish a baseline.",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm list -A -o json | jq -r '.[] | \"\\(.namespace)/\\(.name) chart=\\(.chart) appVersion=\\(.app_version) status=\\(.status)\"'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "helm list -A --failed -o json | jq 'length'",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Detect Helm releases with pending or failed status",
+        "description": "Find releases that are not in a 'deployed' state, which may indicate failed upgrades or stuck rollbacks.",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm list -A --pending -o json | jq -r '.[] | \"PENDING: \\(.namespace)/\\(.name) chart=\\(.chart)\"'",
+          "helm list -A --failed -o json | jq -r '.[] | \"FAILED: \\(.namespace)/\\(.name) chart=\\(.chart) updated=\\(.updated)\"'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check for outdated container images",
+        "description": "List all unique container images in the cluster and flag any using mutable tags like 'latest' or missing a tag entirely.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .spec.containers[*]}{.image}{\"\\n\"}{end}{end}' | sort -u | grep -E ':latest$|^[^:]+$' || echo 'No mutable-tag images found'"
+        ],
+        "validation": {
+          "command": "kubectl get pods -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .spec.containers[*]}{.image}{\"\\n\"}{end}{end}' | sort -u | grep -cE ':latest$|^[^:]+$' || echo 0",
+          "expected": "0"
+        }
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Detect image version inconsistencies across namespaces",
+        "description": "Find cases where the same base image is running different versions in different namespaces, indicating drift.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace} {range .spec.containers[*]}{.image}{\"\\n\"}{end}{end}' | awk -F'[/: ]' '{base=$(NF-1); tag=$NF; key=base} {versions[key]=versions[key]\" \"$1\"=\"tag} END {for(k in versions) print k\": \"versions[k]}' | sort"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Summarize Helm chart age",
+        "description": "Show how long ago each Helm release was last updated to identify releases that may need attention.",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm list -A -o json | jq -r '.[] | \"\\(.namespace)/\\(.name) updated=\\(.updated)\"' | sort -t= -k2"
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Version drift check complete. All Helm releases and container images have been reviewed for staleness, mutable tags, and cross-namespace inconsistencies.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm list -A",
+        "kubectl get pods -A -o jsonpath='{..image}' | tr ' ' '\\n' | sort -u"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": ["orbit", "maintenance", "recurring", "version-drift", "helm", "images", "upgrades"],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "operations",
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": ["maintenance"]
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": ["kubectl", "helm", "jq"],
+    "description": "Requires kubectl and helm CLI with cluster access. jq is needed for JSON parsing."
+  },
+  "orbitConfig": {
+    "cadence": "weekly",
+    "orbitType": "version-drift"
+  }
+}
